calendar.matters:
MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar May-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION

Appearances: Matthew S. Covington of Buchalter (415.227.3517) for defendants and moving parties; Andrew F. Kim of Kim Riley Law (818.216.5288) appearing via Zoom for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the court modifies its ruling as follows: motion to compel arbitration is granted. The matter is stayed pending the conclusion of arbitration proceedings. The court grants plaintiff's request to stay the efficacy of the order for 60 days.

Plaintiff Brees Company, Inc. filed this lawsuit alleging, among other things, that defendants breached the option agreement. Plaintiff attached the option agreement to its complaint. The option agreement is a short document which references a 2018 equity plan in numerous places including in the title of the option agreement. The option agreement explicitly provides that: 1) the 2018 equity plan "govern[s] ... [plaintiff's] rights and obligations under the Option;" 2) "the Option and the shares ... are subject to restrictions on transfer as contained in ... the [2018] Plan; 3) plaintiff "acknowlege[s]" that he was provided with a copy of the [2018] Plan; and 4) plaintiff was advised to read the 2018 Plan "carefully." The foregoing shows that the 2018 equity plan is a key part of the Option Agreement.

It is undisputed that there was and remains no 2018 equity plan and that the only equity plans that defendants have issued are a 2016 equity plan and a 2019 equity plan and that both of those plans provide for arbitration of all disputes relating to those plans. Under these circumstances, the references to a 2018 equity plan in the Option Agreement were a mistake and that those references were objectively intended to be to either the 2016 equity plan or the 2019 equity plan. For purposes of this motion, the court need not decide which of those two plans was objectively intended to be referenced in the Option Agreement.

Based on the objective intentions of the parties, the court finds that there is a valid and enforceable agreement between the parties requiring the arbitration of the claims alleged by the plaintiff.

The court grants plaintiff's request to rule on its Request for Judcial Notice and evidentiary objections. As to the Request for Judicial Notice, the request is granted as to exhibit 1, exhibit 2-5 (not the truth of the contents), exhibit 11, exhibit 12. The request is denied as to exhibits 6-10, exhibits 13-14 (not properly judicially noticeable).

As to the objections to the Declaration of Timo Ahopelto, the court rules as follows: Sustained as to objections 1, 2 and 5. Overruled as to 3.

As to the objections to the Declaration of David Shuman, the court rules as follows: Sustained as to 1, overruled as to 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 and exhibits A, B and C.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Ryan Wheeler, CSR 13717 (T: 760.805.3974, E: ryanwheeler91@yahoo.com) (301/HEK)

MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
Off Calendar May-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS' MOTION STAY PROCEEDINGS

Appearances: Matthew S. Covington of Buchalter (415.227.3517) for defendants and moving parties; Andrew F. Kim of Kim Riley Law (818.216.5288) appearing via Zoom for plaintiff and responding party.

This matter is not argued. The court modifies its ruling as follows: Defendants' Oura Health Oy and Ouraring Inc.'s motion to stay proceedings is moot. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Ryan Wheeler, CSR 13717 (T: 760.805.3974, E: ryanwheeler91@yahoo.com) (301/HEK)
